text: Title: The Multiple Perspectives of Islamic Law on Early Marriage This study is aimed at investigating the pros and cons of early marriage (marriage with minors under 18 years old) from the multiple perspectives of Islamic law. This study was carried out using qualitative research method with library research approach and content analysis. The authors analyzed the contents of numerous primary and secondary sources of Islamic law from the four schools of thought, theological, legal and authorship aspects. The results showed that in Islamic tradition, the law of marriage is considered as a union of worship, morality and a flexible sharia law which serves to maintain the individual’s psychological and sexual well-being. The practice of early marriage is a controversy among scholars. Although the practice is allowed by some scholars who are based on the argumentation of the prophetic example of Muhammad’s marriage to Aisyah, nowadays there is a trend of controversy in Islamic world between the classical Islamic law and the contemporary socio-legal perspective. In Indonesia, the Marriage Act (UUP) and the Compilation of Islamic Law (KHI) have regulated the minimum age for marrying to protect the physical, psychological and economically maturity of the minors. The study found that early marriage had many negative consequences such as increase in divorce rate, health problems and emotional instability. Therefore, this study concludes by suggesting a comprehensive legal and Shariah perspective in protecting the rights of minors in line with the Maqasid al-Shari'ah.
